summary: Iteration-5 validation of CricPose AI Pro (CSV endpoints, dashboard upgrade, MultiProRadar overlay on Compare, premium UI). Backend: 14/14 runnable pytest cases pass (health, full auth flow, dashboard shape with score_trend+latest, 7 compare profiles incl. Cummins, CSV unknown→400 + auth, upload validations, reports 404 + list-auth). 12 upload-dependent tests auto-skipped because no real bowling MP4 clip is available and MediaPipe rejects synthetic clips with 'No bowler detected' 422. Frontend: Landing, ProtectedRoute redirect, login, Dashboard (all 8 required data-testids present), Navbar navigation across Dashboard/Analysis/Compare/History/Settings, and Compare page shell all render correctly in premium dark theme. MultiProRadar overlay + chips are correctly gated behind a submitted comparison result and therefore not visible without a saved report — UX-correct but not exercised in this iteration. Upload→AnalysisDashboard (15 metric tiles, 5 video tabs, 4 BoxPlots, Symmetry, RiskHeatmap, JointTrajectory, 4 BarComparisons, CSV/PDF buttons) and ?report= preselect flows could not be executed end-to-end because no real bowling video is available to seed a report.

issues: Recharts logs 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' warnings on dashboard with 0 sessions — cosmetic (ResponsiveContainer inside an initially 0-height flex panel until data arrives). Consider rendering an explicit empty-state placeholder instead of the chart wrapper when score_trend is empty.

action_items: Provide (or commit to /app/backend/tests/fixtures/) a short real-person bowling-like MP4 clip so CSV metrics/motion/events content, /video+/tracking+/sidebyside+/slowmo 206 streaming, PDF magic-byte check, AnalysisDashboard 15-tile render, and ?report= preselect flows can be fully exercised by the next testing pass.
Optional: suppress Recharts width/height warnings on empty-state dashboard by conditionally rendering the chart only when score_trend.length > 0.
critical_code_review_comments: analysis.py CSV export loads `from fastapi.responses import Response` inside the handler — move to module-level import for consistency.

PRECREATED_REPORT_ID is hard-coded in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (iteration-4 id '69eb491cab8a5e7440652153') and will not exist in fresh DBs; tests TestNewVideoEndpoints/TestPremiumMetrics will fail with 404 instead of skip. New test_cricpose_iter5.py replaces these with dynamic report-id resolution + graceful skip.

Compare page: multi-pro-overlay-panel only mounts after user submits compare (requires both a saved analysis and a successful POST /api/compare). This is fine UX but means the overlay chips/SVG are untestable until at least one analysis exists — consider mounting the panel alongside the selector so users can explore pros even before uploading.

Upload endpoint returns 422 on MediaPipe detection failure. Per FastAPI conventions a body/validation error is 422 but a pipeline/feature error is usually 400/500; the spec says 'Please upload a clearer delivery clip' which is really a 400 user-input error. Cosmetic.

success_rate.backend: 100% of runnable (14/14); 12/26 skipped due to no bowling clip available
success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all visible flows verified; MultiProRadar overlay/chips and AnalysisDashboard post-upload not exercised)

test_credentials: test@cricpose.ai / test1234 (works); coach@cricpose.ai / cricpose2026 seeded; guest login endpoint verified.
seed_data_creation: None. /tmp/demo.mp4 generated synthetically but rejected by MediaPipe pose detection (422 'No bowler detected'). Mongo test_database has 0 reports for test@cricpose.ai after running suite.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL is https://cricpose-ai-pro.preview.emergentagent.com (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Use /app/backend/tests/test_cricpose_iter5.py — it auto-signs-up the seeded athlete if missing and auto-skips any report-dependent test when no reports exist. To unlock the 12 skipped tests, drop a real single-camera bowling MP4 at /tmp/demo.mp4 (or seed a report via POST /api/analysis/upload) and the fixture `any_report_id` will pick it up. All 8 dashboard data-testids are wired correctly. Compare page overlay panel is gated behind a successful POST /api/compare response — to reach it you need at least one saved analysis then select it in the dropdown and submit.
rca of the issue: Upload-dependent tests (CSV content, video 206 streaming, PDF magic bytes, AnalysisDashboard render, ?report= preselect overlay) cannot be exercised because (1) no real bowling clip is bundled with the repo, (2) MediaPipe's bowler-detector correctly rejects synthetic OpenCV-generated clips with 'No bowler detected' 422, and (3) the previous PRECREATED_REPORT_ID used by iteration-4's backend_test.py does not exist in this freshly ported Mongo instance. Mitigation: switched to a new test file that resolves report ids dynamically and gracefully skips when none exist, so the suite reports a clean pass instead of a false-positive failure wall.
